react.answer:
## Building a Breadcrumbs Component in React **Mental model:** Breadcrumbs are an ordered list of links ending in the current page (which is plain text, not a link). The one interesting twist is *collapsing* the middle when the trail is long — keep the first crumb and the last few, replacing the rest with an ellipsis. ### Step 1 — Semantic structure Use `
` wrapping an `
    `; the last crumb gets `aria-current="page"`. ```jsx
      {/* crumbs */}
    ``` ### Step 2 — Render link vs current Every crumb but the last is a link; the last is non-interactive text. ```jsx const isLast = i === shown.length - 1; isLast ? {crumb.label} : {crumb.label} ``` ### Step 3 — Collapse the middle When the trail exceeds `maxItems`, keep the first crumb and the last `maxItems - 1`, and mark a gap. ```jsx let shown = items, collapsed = false; if (maxItems > 0 && items.length > maxItems) { shown = [items[0], ...items.slice(items.length - (maxItems - 1))]; collapsed = true; } ``` ### Step 4 — Separators between crumbs Render the separator before every crumb except the first (and an ellipsis after the first when collapsed). ```jsx {i > 0 && {separator}} ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Single crumb | Renders as current page, no separators. | | Collapse disabled | `maxItems = 0` shows everything. | | Separator semantics | `aria-hidden` so screen readers skip the glyph. | **Interview tip:** Marking the final crumb with `aria-current="page"` and hiding separators with `aria-hidden` is the accessibility nuance interviewers look for — breadcrumbs are an easy place to show you know ARIA beyond just slapping on roles.

vue.answer:
## Building a Breadcrumbs Component in Vue 3 **Mental model:** Breadcrumbs are an ordered list of links ending in the current page (plain text, not a link). The interesting part is collapsing a long trail: keep the first crumb and the last few, replacing the middle with an ellipsis. A `computed` derives the displayed crumbs from the props. ### Step 1 — Derive the shown crumbs A `computed` keeps the collapse logic out of the template. ```js const shown = computed(() => { if (props.maxItems > 0 && props.items.length > props.maxItems) { return [props.items[0], ...props.items.slice(props.items.length - (props.maxItems - 1))]; } return props.items; }); const collapsed = computed(() => shown.value.length < props.items.length); ``` ### Step 2 — Semantic structure `
` around an `
    `; the last crumb is current. ```vue
      ...
    ``` ### Step 3 — Link vs current The last crumb is text with `aria-current="page"`; the rest are links. ```vue {{ crumb.label }} {{ crumb.label }} ``` ### Step 4 — Separators and ellipsis Render a separator before each non-first crumb, and an ellipsis after the first when collapsed. ```vue {{ separator }} ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Single crumb | Current page, no separators. | | Collapse disabled | `maxItems = 0` shows everything. | | Reactivity | `computed` re-derives when `items`/`maxItems` change. | **Interview tip:** Putting the collapse logic in a `computed` (not inline in the template or a method) is the idiomatic Vue choice — it's cached and re-runs only when its reactive deps change.

angular.answer:
## Building a Breadcrumbs Component in Angular **Mental model:** Breadcrumbs are an ordered list of links ending in the current page (plain text, not a link). The interesting part is collapsing a long trail — keep the first crumb and the last few, replacing the middle with an ellipsis. A getter derives the displayed crumbs. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Derive the shown crumbs A getter keeps the collapse logic out of the template. ```typescript get shown(): Crumb[] { if (this.maxItems > 0 && this.items.length > this.maxItems) { return [this.items[0], ...this.items.slice(this.items.length - (this.maxItems - 1))]; } return this.items; } get collapsed(): boolean { return this.shown.length < this.items.length; } ``` ### Step 2 — Semantic structure `
` around an `
    `; the last crumb is current. ```html
      ...
    ``` ### Step 3 — Link vs current Switch on the last index with `*ngIf`/`else`. ```html {{ crumb.label }} {{ crumb.label }} ``` ### Step 4 — Separators and ellipsis Render a separator before each non-first crumb, and an ellipsis after the first when collapsed. ```html {{ separator }} ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Single crumb | Current page, no separators. | | Collapse disabled | `maxItems = 0` shows everything. | | Separator semantics | `aria-hidden` so screen readers skip the glyph. | **Interview tip:** Note the `*ngIf … ; else` + `` pattern for the link-vs-current branch — it's Angular's idiomatic ternary for templates and avoids duplicating the crumb markup.
